notes: Author; joined PHI in March 1969 at age 21 as a junior programmer earning $7,200/year.
Service bureau headquartered in a converted funeral home in Arlington, Massachusetts. Founders had Apollo program backgrounds. Acquired by Wang Laboratories circa 1972.
Founder of PHI; had background writing navigation code for the Apollo lunar program.
Engineering lead at PHI; Apollo program veteran; later provided key technical leadership for Dr. An Wang's computing initiatives after acquisition.
Senior programmer and mentor to Kastner at PHI; later architected the Wang VS operating system. Taught resource-efficiency philosophy.
Acquired PHI circa 1972; relocated professional staff to Tewksbury, Massachusetts facility.
Founder of Wang Laboratories; David Moros provided key technical leadership for his computing initiatives post-acquisition.
Cambridge, Massachusetts consultancy where Kastner moved after leaving PHI; several PHI alumni joined together.
PHI colleague whom Kastner followed to Arthur D. Little Systems after the Wang acquisition.
PHI client in July 1969; Kastner upgraded their bond portfolio system and fixed an early Y2K two-digit-year bug.
New England gas station and convenience store chain; early PHI client for whom Kastner built a retail accounting system handling pump counter rollover at 9,999 gallons.
